zh.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mains是Robert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and Drumgrey和Dama En Blanco的兒子，於大約1435年出生在英國蘇格蘭。他於大約1526年與Margaret Angus在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾凱爾拉維洛可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1502年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mains是Robert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and Drumgrey和Dama En Blanco的儿子，于大约1435年出生在英国苏格兰。他于大约1526年与Margaret Angus在英国苏格兰度福利爾凱爾拉維洛可结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1502年之后。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: IVth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Swiss German: possibly from a short form of surnames formed with Id-, Ith-, the root of ancient Germanic origin, of disputed etymology, possibly cognate with Old Norse idh ‘work, activity’.
Cambodian: written អ៊ិត, unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Mains
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French mayns, i.e. ‘with the hands’.
Scottish: variant of Main , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Scottish: habitational name from the frequent Scots local word or placename mains ‘demesne farm’.

variants.name: Maines
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Mains  .

variants.name: Main
meanings: Scottish: from a shortened form of the Scandinavian personal name Magnus , which in Older Scots became Manus and Mane.
English (of Norman origin) and French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Ma(g)ino, Meino, from magan ‘strength, might’.
English (of Norman origin): nickname for a large man, from Old French magne, maine ‘great, big’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mains was born about 1435, in Scotland, United Kingdom as the son of Robert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and Drumgrey and Dama En Blanco. He married Margaret Angus about 1526, in Caerlaverock, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died after 1502.

fr.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mains est né environ 1435, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fils de Robert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and Drumgrey et Dama En Blanco. Il a épousé Margaret Angus environ 1526, à Caerlaverock, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé après 1502.

de.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mains wurde um 1435 in Schottland, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Robert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and Drumgrey und Dama En Blanco geboren. Er hat um 1526 in Caerlaverock, Dumfriesshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Margaret Angus geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb nach 1502.

it.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mains è nato il circa 1435 nel Scozia, Regno Unito come figlio di Robert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and Drumgrey e Dama En Blanco. Lui sposato Margaret Angus circa 1526, nel Caerlaverock, Dumfriesshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì dopo il 1502.

ja.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mainsは約1435年にイギリススコットランドでRobert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and DrumgreyとDama En Blancoの息子として生まれた。約1526年にイギリススコットランドダムフリーシュアイアケエルラバーオックでMargaret Angusと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1502年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mains(은)는 대략 1435년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Robert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and Drumgrey(와)과 Dama En Blanco의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1526년 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르 카이를레이베록에서 Margaret Angus(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1502년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mains nasceu aproximadamente 1435, em Escócia, United Kingdom filho de Robert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and Drumgrey e Dama En Blanco. Ele casou-se com Margaret Angus aproximadamente 1526, em Caerlaverock, Dumfriesshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu após 1502.

ru.lifeSummary: William Douglas IVth of Mains родился около 1435 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Robert Charteris 5th of Amisfield and Drumgrey и Dama En Blanco. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Angus около 1526 г. в местности каирлавирокк, думфриессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер после 1502 г.
